{{Quote|Lookit the way she's holdin' that lightsaber, Gudb. Think maybe she's a ''Jedi''?|Rek, to Gudb, referring to Nomi Sunrider|<ref name="nomi1" />}}
'''Rek''' was a tall [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Gangster/Legends|gangster]] who worked as an enforcer for the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Bogga]] in the years leading up to the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. Rek was a member of [[Bogga's enforcer gang]], which operated out of the [[Stenness Node]] region of the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]]. In [[3999 BBY]], a shipment of valuable [[Adegan crystal/Legends|Adegan crystals]], in the possession of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Andur Sunrider]], made its way onto the [[Stenness Hyperspace Terminal]]. Determined to obtain the jewels, Bogga sent Rek, along with fellow enforcers [[Quanto]] and [[Gudb]], to get the [[Crystal/Legends|crystals]]. Rek and Quanto created a diversion by knocking over the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi's]] [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]], [[A-3DO]]. Sunrider, who had brought the crystals to the [[Space station/Legends|station]], was distracted by the altercation, allowing Gudb to release his pet [[Gorm-worm/Legends|gorm-worm]], [[Skritch]], who [[Death/Legends|fatally]] bit the Jedi Knight. However, Sunrider's grieving wife, [[Nomi Sunrider|Nomi]], took up her fallen husband's [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], surprising Rek. In self-defense, Nomi attacked the three enforcers, slaying Rek as he drew his [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Hey! Watch where yer goin', droid!|Rek, to A-3DO|<ref name="nomi1" />}}
Rek, a tall [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]], was a criminal [[Bogga's enforcer gang|enforcer]] affiliated with the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Bogga]] in the years leading up the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. Rek was one of Bogga's top men, and he accompanied the Hutt along with several other enforcers when Bogga stayed at the [[Stenness Hyperspace Terminal]] [[Space station/Legends|space station]] near the [[Ambria system/Legends|Stenness system]]. In [[3999 BBY]], word reached Bogga that a shipment of highly valuable [[Adegan crystal/Legends|Adegan crystals]] had made its way onto the station, and the Hutt decided that he needed to obtain the jewels at any cost. Bogga sent three of his enforcers—Rek, [[Quanto]], and [[Gudb]]—to steal the [[Crystal/Legends|crystals]]. However, Bogga warned the three that the possessor of the crystals, [[Andur Sunrider]], was a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. Rek, along with Quanto and Gudb, made his way to the [[Hangar/Legends|docking bay]], where Sunrider had landed his [[Lightside Explorer|ship]]. The three eventually came across Sunrider; his wife, [[Nomi Sunrider|Nomi]]; daughter, [[Vima Sunrider|Vima]]; and [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]], [[A-3DO]]. To create a diversion, Rek purposefully bumped into A-3DO and told Quanto to destroy the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] for getting in his way. This commotion drew the attention of Andur Sunrider, who came over to assist his droid. While the Jedi was distracted, Gudb released his pet [[Gorm-worm/Legends|gorm-worm]], [[Skritch]], who [[Death/Legends|fatally]] bit Sunrider.<ref name="nomi1" />

Grieved, Nomi Sunrider knelt over Andur's corpse, only to see his [[Force ghost/Legends|Force ghost]].<ref name="nomi1" /> Nomi began speaking to her husband's ghost, something that piqued Rek's curiosity, as he could not see the ghost and it seemed to him that she was talking to the air.<ref name="audio">[[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]]</ref> The specter of the Jedi directed Nomi to pick up her fallen husband's [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] in self-defense. This action surprised Rek, who conjectured that Nomi could possibly be a Jedi as well. Suddenly, Nomi [[Sai tok/Legends|cut Quanto in half]], and Rek was slain as well as he drew his [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]].<ref name="nomi1" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Blast him, Quanto. Space junk shouldn't ''talk back'' to flesh an' blood!|Rek|<ref name="nomi1" />}}
Rek was a gruff, imposing enforcer who decided to make his living through crime. Rek held a disdain for droids, referring to them as "space junk" and believing that they had no right talking back to flesh-and-[[Blood/Legends|blood]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]].<ref name="nomi1" /> After the death of Andur Sunrider, Rek found Nomi Sunrider's behavior odd, as it seemed to him that she was merely speaking to the air when she was in fact talking to her husband's [[The Force/Legends|Force]] ghost. Rek was also surprised when she took up her husband's lightsaber, and he reacted angrily to Nomi's slaying of Quanto before being killed himself.<ref name="audio" />

==Behind the scenes==
Rek first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Saga of Nomi Sunrider]]'', the second story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[Tom Veitch]] and released in [[1993]]. Rek only appears in the arc's [[Tales of the Jedi 3|first issue]], in which he is killed. In this appearance, Rek is illustrated by [[Janine Johnston]].<ref name="nomi1" /> Rek was also featured in the [[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|audio adaptation]] of these stories, in which he was voiced by [[Tony Sperry]].<ref name="audio" />
